narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after all the effort you've put into your law school dreams, and I want to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to the bar exam as riskier than we could support—it's not about you as a person, but the patterns it spotted in your profile. At the top of what stood out was your Year 3 decile of 1.0, placing you in the bottom tenth of your school that year, which was the strongest signal pulling toward a higher chance of bar failure, as it suggested challenges sustaining performance over time. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 24.0, which the model flagged as notably low compared to those who typically pass, making it a big factor in the 60% failure probability it estimated for you. Your Year 1 decile of 1.0 echoed that early struggle, reinforcing a picture of consistent academic hurdles right from the start. Layered on top was your lower-middle family income, which often correlates with fewer resources for the intense bar prep many successful students rely on. Your Asian background played a role too, as the model noted patterns where it sometimes ties to higher competition and pressure in these high-stakes exams. Even your solid undergraduate GPA of 3.0 couldn't fully offset things, pulling somewhat toward failure in this context, while being male and planning full-time study rounded out a profile that, in combination, painted you as facing steeper odds. It's heartbreaking because we see your determination, but these elements together just tipped the scales for the model, leading to this tough call on your application. The good news? This isn't destiny—you can target those weak spots like boosting test prep or seeking scholarships to shift your story for the next opportunity. Hang in there; your journey's far from over.
